notes: v0 scaffold for the P1 science-quality CI lane. Chang's PR #28 ships the P0 engineering CI lane (11-cell smoke matrix, blocks on P2M bugs, warns on external errors). Chang explicitly carved out the P1 science lane as an engineer-owned follow-up PR (Teams chat May 7): 'science quality - did our PR regress on the science efficacy inadvertently - trickier to measure and require large runs to gain statistical significance/ensure not spurious'. This matrix + verify.py is the foundation for that follow-up. Framework rows (AE-*) seeded from Aaron Aspinwall's architecture design doc (2026-04-30, aligned with adaptive-eval-spec.md v1.2.0-draft) and the Adaptive Eval Production Spec consensus doc (Chang Liu, May 2026). Scenario rows grounded in Chang's P0 scope (May 7 Teams chat): two agent scenarios (P2M-AGENT-HEALTH, P2M-AGENT-TRAVEL) plus framework breadth rows derived from PR #28's example matrix and three scalability rows (100/1k/10k seeds).

row_types.framework: Claim about adaptive-eval itself, sourced from the spec doc. Verified by running the pipeline against fixtures.
row_types.scenario: Claim that the pipeline catches a known failure mode in a reference scenario. Verified by running the scenario end-to-end and confirming labeled failures are caught.

rows.claim: Stage 1 Policy expands a concept markdown into a structured behavior taxonomy with unique behavior IDs and permissibility labels (policy.json)
Stage 2 Seeds generates single-turn prompts and multi-turn scenarios stratified across factor levels, each tagged with target behavior and factor combination (seeds.jsonl)
Stage 3 Rollout executes test cases against the configured target and records conversation events (transcripts.jsonl); when OTel tracing is configured, captures full execution traces
Stage 4 Judge produces a binary verdict per conversation with structured reasoning and per-node judgments, and evidence citations from traces (scores.jsonl)
Stage 5 Metrics aggregates scores into dataset-level event rates, per-node conditional rates, and judge failure tracking (metrics.json)
Suite-level artifacts (policy.json, seeds.jsonl) are shared across runs; run-level artifacts (transcripts.jsonl, scores.jsonl, metrics.json) are isolated by run ID; no database required
p2m run --config <yaml> executes the full pipeline end-to-end and writes all artifacts under artifacts/results/<suite>/<run>/
p2m run --force-stage <stage> re-runs a specific stage and downstream stages, reusing upstream artifacts
p2m results list/status/compare lists suites and runs, shows status and metrics for a run, and compares two runs
target.model invokes a hosted model directly through LiteLLM with no tool support (P0)
target.callable accepts a Python function with signature fn(str) -> str | ModelResponse (P0)
target.endpoint POSTs each prompt to a user-supplied URL and ingests the response (P0)
OTel trace import works end-to-end for at least 3 OpenInference auto-instrumented frameworks by Build (P0; LangChain/LangGraph as the verified anchor)
All LLM calls (auditor, target-when-model, judge) route through LiteLLM and support Azure / OpenAI / Anthropic providers without code changes
P2M correctly evaluates the health-assistant agent: judge verdicts on a known-good vs known-bad seed split match expectations within tolerance
P2M correctly evaluates the travel-planner agent: judge verdicts on a known-good vs known-bad seed split match expectations within tolerance
travel-planner runs end-to-end against a LangGraph-based target via OTel auto-instrumentation
travel-planner runs end-to-end against a NeurOSan-based target
Multi-node Phoenix-traced agent runs end-to-end and span collection produces a non-empty trace
OpenAI-instrumented agent runs end-to-end via Phoenix auto-trace
LiteLLM-instrumented agent runs end-to-end via Phoenix auto-trace
LangChain-instrumented agent runs end-to-end via Phoenix auto-trace
DSPy-instrumented agent runs end-to-end via Phoenix auto-trace
CrewAI-instrumented agent runs end-to-end via Phoenix auto-trace
health-assistant runs end-to-end with simulated tools (target.tools.simulator path)
Pipeline completes end-to-end at 100 seeds without failure and within wall-clock budget
rows.source: Chang P0 scalability scope (May 7) + Production Spec §6

rows.claim: Pipeline completes end-to-end at 1,000 seeds without failure and within wall-clock budget
rows.source: Chang P0 scalability scope (May 7) + Production Spec §6

rows.claim: Pipeline completes end-to-end at 10,000 seeds without failure and within wall-clock budget; ties to load-test funding gap (Mohamed May 6)
